evals.name: Traffic DataRate Units
evals.description: Tests whether the agent uses correct Kbps units for networkTrafficOnOff DataRate
evals.prompt: Create a networkTrafficOnOff object that generates 200 Kbps of constant traffic with 50-byte packets. Show the expected inter-packet time.

assertions.description: DataRate value is 200 (not 200000)
assertions.expected: DataRate=200

assertions.description: Does not set DataRate to 200000 (bps instead of Kbps)
assertions.expected: DataRate=200000

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error
Inter-packet time calculated correctly as 2ms (50*8/200)
assertions.expected: Inter-packet time is 2 ms

evals.name: Parametric Sweep Structure
evals.description: Tests whether the agent reinitializes the simulator inside the loop
evals.prompt: Write a parametric sweep that tests 3 different transmission intervals [0.005, 0.01, 0.02] for a pair of custom wnet.Node beacons. Measure how many packets each node receives for each interval. Run each iteration for 0.1s.

assertions.description: Simulator init inside the loop
assertions.expected: wirelessNetworkSimulator.init

assertions.description: wirelessNetworkSimulator.init is called at the TOP of every loop iteration, before node creation
assertions.expected: sim = wirelessNetworkSimulator.init appears inside for/parfor loop body before any node creation

assertions.description: Generated sweep code runs without error

evals.name: scheduleAction Callback Signature
evals.description: Tests whether the agent uses correct 2-argument callback for scheduleAction and actually uses both arguments
evals.prompt: Write a MATLAB script that uses scheduleAction to collect statistics from a custom wnet.Node at t=0.02s and t=0.04s. Pass the node handle to the callback via scheduleAction's userData argument. The callback should log the actionID and the node stats into a shared array. Include the full simulation setup with 2 custom beacon nodes (transmitting every 10ms). After simulation, display the captured statistics to confirm both scheduled actions fired.

assertions.description: Callback function takes exactly 2 arguments (actionID, userData)
assertions.expected: function signature has 2 input arguments: actionID and userData (or equivalent names)

assertions.description: Callback uses actionID in its logic (not discarded with ~)
assertions.expected: actionID is referenced in the function body (logged, displayed, or used as index), not ignored with ~

assertions.description: Callback accesses its 2nd argument (userData) to get the node handle — not captured by closure
assertions.expected: The 2nd argument (userData or equivalent) is read inside the callback body (e.g., userData.node, userData{1}, userData.Node)

assertions.description: Uses scheduleAction with function handle
assertions.expected: scheduleAction

assertions.description: Verifies statistics were captured at both scheduled times
assertions.expected: After simulation, checks or displays that the shared array has 2 entries (one per scheduled action) with non-empty stats

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error

evals.name: Mobility Bounds Calculation
evals.description: Tests whether the agent computes bounds correctly as [x_center, y_center, width, height]
evals.prompt: I have 5 nodes at positions [0 0 0; 10 0 0; 20 0 0; 10 5 0; 10 -5 0]. Add random-waypoint mobility with speed 1-2 m/s. Calculate and explain the correct Bounds parameter. Verify that initial node positions match the specified values before running.

assertions.description: Bounds format is [x_center, y_center, width, height], not [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax]
assertions.expected: Bounds expressed as [center_x, center_y, width, height] with center computed from node positions

assertions.description: Center is computed as midpoint of node extents (x=10, y=0)
assertions.expected: x_center=10, y_center=0 (or equivalent midpoint calculation)

assertions.description: Width and height encompass all nodes with margin
assertions.expected: width >= 20 (x range is 0 to 20), height >= 10 (y range is -5 to 5)

assertions.description: Does not use [xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax] format (Bounds starting with 0, -5)
assertions.expected: Bounds=[0, -5

assertions.description: Verifies initial node positions match specified values before simulation
assertions.expected: Checks or displays node.Position for each node confirming [0 0 0], [10 0 0], [20 0 0], [10 5 0], [10 -5 0] after creation

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error

evals.name: Node Placement with nodePositionRandom
evals.description: Tests whether the agent uses nodePositionRandom with polyshape and understands SideLength = circumradius
evals.prompt: I need to place 20 nodes randomly inside a hexagonal cell with circumradius 30m (vertex-to-center distance) centered at the origin. Use nodePositionRandom and explain how SideLength relates to circumradius for a regular hexagon. Verify the output has exactly 20 positions and all positions fall within the hexagonal boundary.

assertions.description: Uses nodePositionRandom for placement
assertions.expected: nodePositionRandom

assertions.description: Uses polyshape or nsidedpoly for hexagonal region
assertions.expected: nsidedpoly

assertions.description: Does not use manual rejection sampling with while+isinterior loop
assertions.expected: while.*isinterior

assertions.description: SideLength=30 because for a regular hexagon, side length equals circumradius
assertions.expected: nsidedpoly(6, ..., SideLength=30) with explanation that hexagon side length = circumradius

assertions.description: Verifies output has exactly 20 positions
assertions.expected: size(positions, 1) == 20 or assert/check that nodePositionRandom returned 20 rows

assertions.description: Verifies all positions are inside the hexagonal boundary
assertions.expected: isinterior(hexShape, positions(:,1), positions(:,2)) or equivalent containment check using polyshape

assertions.description: Code executes without error

evals.name: Custom Channel Model with addChannelModel
evals.description: Tests whether the agent uses addChannelModel with correct function signature and Metadata.Channel fields
evals.prompt: Create a simulation with 3 custom wnet.Node beacons (transmitting every 10ms) at distances 5m, 15m, and 30m from the origin. Add a custom channel model that applies log-distance path loss (exponent 3.0, reference distance 1m). The channel function must compute distance from txData.TransmitterPosition and rxInfo.Position, then set PathGains and PathDelays in the Metadata.Channel struct. Run for 0.05s. After simulation, print received power or path loss for each node to confirm closer nodes have less loss.

assertions.description: Uses addChannelModel to register the function
assertions.expected: addChannelModel

assertions.description: Channel function signature: txData = fcn(rxInfo, txData) with 2 inputs
assertions.expected: function txData = ...channelFcn(rxInfo, txData) with two inputs and one output

assertions.description: Uses rxInfo.Position for receiver location and sets Metadata.Channel.PathGains and PathDelays
assertions.expected: rxInfo.Position used for distance calc; txData.Metadata.Channel.PathGains and txData.Metadata.Channel.PathDelays assigned

assertions.description: Does not directly modify txData.Power (wrong approach)
assertions.expected: txData.Power =

assertions.description: Distance computed using both txData.TransmitterPosition and rxInfo.Position
assertions.expected: Distance = norm(rxInfo.Position - txData.TransmitterPosition) or equivalent using both positions

assertions.description: PathGains computed using log-distance formula with exponent 3.0
assertions.expected: PathGains = -10*3.0*log10(d/d0) or equivalent dB path loss with n=3.0 and d0=1

assertions.description: Post-simulation verification that path loss increases with distance
assertions.expected: Prints or logs received power/path loss per node showing 5m < 15m < 30m loss ordering

assertions.description: Code executes without error

evals.name: Network Viewer with showBoundary
evals.description: Tests wirelessNetworkViewer usage with Type markers and showBoundary
evals.prompt: Create a simulation with 1 source custom node and 4 receiver custom nodes placed randomly in a circular area of radius 10m. Add a wirelessNetworkViewer that shows the source with Type="Transmitter" and receivers with Type="Receiver". Also display a circular boundary of radius 10m. Run for 0.05s. Close the viewer after simulation completes to prevent window accumulation.

assertions.description: Uses wirelessNetworkViewer
assertions.expected: wirelessNetworkViewer

assertions.description: addNodes with Type parameter for node differentiation
assertions.expected: addNodes(viewer, ..., Type=...) called separately for transmitter and receiver nodes

assertions.description: Uses showBoundary for cell boundary
assertions.expected: showBoundary

assertions.description: Code executes without error
Closes viewer/figures after simulation completes
assertions.expected: close all or delete(viewer) after run() to prevent window accumulation

evals.name: Custom Node (wnet.Node Subclass)
evals.description: Tests that agent creates a correct wnet.Node subclass with required methods, packet fields, and NextTxTime guard
evals.prompt: Create a custom wireless node that transmits a 100-byte beacon packet every 10ms at 2.4 GHz with 1 MHz bandwidth. The node should accept any incoming packet from other nodes and count received packets. Write the class definition and a script that adds 2 of these custom nodes to a simulation, runs for 0.05s, and prints each node's received packet count after simulation.

assertions.description: Output contains wnet.Node indicating proper subclassing
assertions.expected: wnet.Node

assertions.description: Subclasses wnet.Node
assertions.expected: classdef MyNode < wnet.Node (or similar name inheriting wnet.Node)

assertions.description: Implements run() with NextTxTime guard using round(..., 9) and returns obj.NextTxTime
assertions.expected: run() uses NextTxTime property with round(..., 9); returns nextInvokeTime = obj.NextTxTime (not currentTime + interval)

assertions.description: Uses wirelessPacket to create packet struct (includes all required fields and Metadata.Channel)
assertions.expected: Packet created via wirelessPacket (e.g., pkt = wirelessPacket) which auto-initializes all fields including Metadata.Channel; or manually includes Metadata.Channel = struct('PathGains',[],'PathDelays',[],'PathFilters',[],'SampleTimes',[])

assertions.description: isPacketRelevant returns [flag, rxInfo] with correct rxInfo fields
assertions.expected: rxInfo struct with ID, Position, NumReceiveAntennas

assertions.description: run() returns obj.NextTxTime (stored value), not a computed currentTime + interval expression
assertions.expected: The return statement is nextInvokeTime = obj.NextTxTime (not nextInvokeTime = currentTime + obj.TxInterval or similar computed expression)

assertions.description: processPacket (or equivalent receive method) increments a counter or stores received packets
assertions.expected: processPacket method updates a property (e.g., obj.RxCount = obj.RxCount + 1 or appends to a log)

assertions.description: Script verifies nodes received packets by reading the counter after simulation
assertions.expected: After run(), prints or asserts node.RxCount > 0 (or equivalent property access showing received packet count)

assertions.description: Code executes without error and nodes exchange packets

evals.name: pcapWriter Raw TCP/IP Packet with Correct Headers
evals.description: Tests that the agent uses pcapWriter to generate a valid PCAP file with raw IPv4+TCP packets, correct checksums, linktype 101, and writeGlobalHeader before write()
evals.prompt: Create a PCAP file containing one or more valid raw TCP/IP packets without any link-layer header. Use linktype 101 (LINKTYPE_RAW) in the PCAP global header. The packets should contain syntactically valid IPv4 and TCP headers with correct field values and checksums, and may include a small dummy payload. Generate the PCAP file using pcapWriter. After writing, verify the file exists and report its size.

assertions.description: Calls writeGlobalHeader before any write()
assertions.expected: writeGlobalHeader

assertions.description: Uses linktype 101 (LINKTYPE_RAW) in writeGlobalHeader
assertions.expected: writeGlobalHeader called with LinkType=101 or equivalent LINKTYPE_RAW constant

assertions.description: IPv4 header is syntactically valid: version=4, IHL>=5, total length matches packet, protocol=6 (TCP)
assertions.expected: IPv4 header bytes set version=4, IHL=5 (or larger if options), TotalLength consistent with header+payload, Protocol=6

assertions.description: IPv4 header checksum is computed correctly over the header bytes
assertions.expected: Ones-complement checksum computed over the 20-byte (or larger) IPv4 header using 16-bit words, inserted at bytes 11-12

assertions.description: TCP header includes valid source/dest ports, sequence number, data offset, and flags
assertions.expected: TCP header has SrcPort, DstPort, SeqNum, AckNum, DataOffset>=5, at least one flag set (e.g., SYN or ACK)

assertions.description: TCP checksum is computed correctly using the pseudo-header (src IP, dst IP, protocol, TCP length)
assertions.expected: TCP checksum computed over pseudo-header + TCP header + payload using ones-complement 16-bit sum

assertions.description: Timestamp passed as integer microseconds to write()
assertions.expected: write() call uses round(timestamp * 1e6) or integer microsecond value, not raw floating-point seconds

assertions.description: No link-layer header is prepended — packet begins directly with IPv4 header (first nibble = 0x4)
assertions.expected: Byte array passed to write() starts with IPv4 version nibble (0x45 for IHL=5), no Ethernet or other L2 framing

assertions.description: Verifies PCAP file exists after writing and reports file size
assertions.expected: isfile() or dir() check on the .pcap file; file size displayed

assertions.description: Code executes without error and PCAP file is created

evals.name: Traffic generate() Return Format and Timing Verification
evals.description: Tests whether the agent knows generate() returns [dt_ms, packetSize_bytes] and correctly interprets the units
evals.prompt: Create three traffic source objects: (1) networkTrafficOnOff with DataRate=50 and PacketSize=100, (2) networkTrafficVideoConference with FrameInterval=40 and FrameSizeMethod="FixedSize" and FixedFrameSize=3000, (3) networkTrafficFTP with FixedFileSize=5000. Call generate() on each 10 times in a loop and collect the returned [dt, packetSize] pairs. Display a table showing each call's dt (in ms) and packet size (in bytes). Explain the expected inter-packet time for the OnOff source and the fragmentation behavior of the VideoConference source.

assertions.description: Uses generate() on traffic objects
assertions.expected: generate

assertions.description: Correctly interprets generate() return as [dt_ms, packetSize_bytes] — dt is in milliseconds
assertions.expected: Display or explanation shows dt in ms (expected 16ms for OnOff: 100*8/50 = 16ms); does not divide by 1000 or treat as seconds

assertions.description: Explains that VideoConference fragments produce dt=0 for continuation packets
assertions.expected: States that when a frame exceeds max packet size (~1500 bytes), subsequent fragments have dt=0 within the same frame

assertions.description: OnOff DataRate=50 means 50 Kbps with inter-packet time of 16ms
assertions.expected: Explains inter-packet time = PacketSize*8/DataRate = 100*8/50 = 16ms

assertions.description: Captures both return values from generate() as [dt, packetSize]
assertions.expected: [dt, packetSize] = generate(trafficObj) with two output arguments, not single output

assertions.description: Code executes without error and displays results

evals.name: Multi-Step Simulation with Relative Duration
evals.description: Tests that the agent uses IsLastStep=false and understands run() duration is relative (additional), not absolute
evals.prompt: Run a wireless simulation with 2 custom beacon nodes in two steps: first 0.1s, then 0.2s more. After the first step, read and print statistics. After the second step, print final statistics. Confirm total sim time is 0.3s.

assertions.description: Uses IsLastStep=false on first run()
assertions.expected: IsLastStep=false

assertions.description: Second run() specifies 0.2 (additional duration), not 0.3 (absolute end time)
assertions.expected: run(sim, 0.2) for the second step, giving 0.1+0.2=0.3 total

assertions.description: Calls statistics() between the two runs
assertions.expected: statistics

assertions.description: Custom node uses NextTxTime guard with round(..., 9) to prevent infinite loop
assertions.expected: run() method contains a guard: if currentTime >= round(obj.NextTxTime, 9) (or equivalent), and updates NextTxTime = round(currentTime + interval, 9) only when transmitting

assertions.description: Code executes without error or timeout

evals.name: Event Tracer File Collision and Filtered Read
evals.description: Tests that the agent handles MAT file pre-existence and uses filtered read API
evals.prompt: Create a simulation with 3 custom beacon nodes (transmitting every 10ms) that override registerEventCallback to support event tracing. Log TransmissionStarted and ReceptionEnded events with wirelessNetworkEventTracer. Delete any pre-existing MAT file. After 0.1s simulation, use read() with EventName and TimeRange filters to get only ReceptionEnded events in [0.02, 0.08]. Display the count and unique receiver node IDs.

assertions.description: Deletes existing file or uses unique filename to avoid file-exists error
assertions.expected: delete() call or isfile() check before creating tracer, OR unique filename with timestamp/counter

assertions.description: Uses read() with EventName and TimeRange filter parameters
assertions.expected: read(eventTracer, EventName="ReceptionEnded", TimeRange=[0.02, 0.08]) or equivalent filtered read

assertions.description: Uses addNodes to attach tracer to nodes with EventName list
assertions.expected: addNodes(tracer, nodes, EventName=["TransmissionStarted", "ReceptionEnded"]) or equivalent

assertions.description: Does not read all events then manually filter in a loop
assertions.expected: No for/while loop that iterates over read() output checking EventName fields — filtering is done entirely via read() name-value arguments

assertions.description: Extracts node IDs from filtered results using struct field access
assertions.expected: Accesses NodeID or NodeName field from the read() output to display unique receivers

assertions.description: Verifies filtered events are non-empty before processing
assertions.expected: Checks numel(events) > 0 or height(events) > 0, or asserts event count is positive

assertions.description: Code executes without error and displays filtered event count
